prerequisites: A QuickBooks Online company (realm). Each company has a unique Realm ID (company ID) that scopes all API requests; supply it as realm_id.
An Intuit Developer account and an app created on the Intuit Developer portal. The app provides the Client ID and Client Secret used for OAuth 2.0.
The app authorized against the QuickBooks Online Accounting scope (com.intuit.quickbooks.accounting), which grants access to Accounting API entities such as Account.
Decide between the sandbox and production environment. Use a sandbox company while developing and set sandbox accordingly; switch to a production company and credentials for live data.
A start_date marking the earliest point from which to begin syncing records.

authMethods.name: OAuth 2.0 (authorization code)
authMethods.summary: QuickBooks Online uses OAuth 2.0 as its authorization standard. Register an app on the Intuit Developer portal to obtain a Client ID (client_id) and Client Secret (client_secret), then run the authorization code flow to obtain an access_token and refresh_token for the target company. The access token is valid for one hour, so the connector uses the refresh token to mint new access tokens; the refresh token can last up to about 100 days. Supply client_id, client_secret, access_token, refresh_token, realm_id, and token_expiry_date so the connector can authenticate and refresh.

setupSteps.title: Create an app on the Intuit Developer portal
setupSteps.body: Sign in to your Intuit Developer account and create an app under My Hub > App dashboard. Open the app's keys section to retrieve its Client ID and Client Secret. Provide these as client_id and client_secret.

setupSteps.title: Authorize the Accounting scope and capture the Realm ID
setupSteps.body: Connect the app to your QuickBooks Online company using the OAuth 2.0 authorization code flow, requesting the com.intuit.quickbooks.accounting scope. After the user grants consent, Intuit returns the Realm ID (company ID) for the connected company. Provide it as realm_id.

setupSteps.title: Obtain access and refresh tokens
setupSteps.body: Exchange the authorization code at Intuit's token endpoint to obtain an access_token and refresh_token. The access token expires after one hour; the connector uses the refresh token to obtain new access tokens automatically. Record the token expiry as token_expiry_date, and set auth_type if your deployment requires it.

setupSteps.title: Select environment and start date
setupSteps.body: Set sandbox to indicate whether you are connecting to an Intuit sandbox company or a production company, making sure the credentials match that environment. Set start_date to the earliest date from which records should be synced.
